subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Mage Components
- **Breach mages** — Multiple unique mages, each with different starting decks, abilities, and breach configurations.
- **Breach cards** — 4 breach cards per mage (some start open, others closed/focused).
- **Player mats** — Track HP, breaches, and ability.
- **Starting cards** — Each mage starts with a small deck of basic gems and sparks.

#### Supply Cards
- **Gems** — Economy cards (provide Aether currency).
- **Relics** — Utility cards with powerful one-time or ongoing effects.
- **Spells** — Damage-dealing cards prepped on breaches and cast on later turns.
- **Supply piles** — 9 supply piles chosen during setup (3 gems, 3 relics, 3 spells typically).

#### Nemesis Components
- **Nemesis mat** — Tracks nemesis HP and special rules.
- **Nemesis deck** — Unique cards including minions, powers, and attacks.
- **Nemesis-specific tokens** — Varies by boss.

#### Shared Components
- **Turn order deck** — Cards determining player and nemesis turn order.
- **Aether tokens** — Currency for buying cards.
- **Damage tokens** — Track damage on nemesis and minions.
- **Life tokens** — Track mage HP.
- **Gravehold mat** — Tracks the city's HP (shared team resource).

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose nemesis** — Select a nemesis boss and set up its mat and HP.
2. **Nemesis deck** — Shuffle the nemesis deck; some nemeses have specific setup instructions.
3. **Set Gravehold HP** — Place Gravehold mat at 30 HP (default).
4. **Choose supply piles** — Select 9 supply piles (gems, relics, spells) from available cards.
5. **Arrange supply** — Place supply piles in a row accessible to all players.
6. **Turn order deck** — Shuffle the turn order cards (player cards + nemesis cards).
7. **Place tokens** — Set damage, life, and Aether tokens in shared supply.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Mage Selection
- **Choose mage** — Each player selects a unique breach mage.
- **Starting deck** — Take the mage's starting deck (typically 5 gems + 4 sparks + 1 unique card).
- **Set HP** — Set life dial to mage's starting HP.
- **Breach layout** — Arrange 4 breach cards as shown on mage mat; some start open, others closed.

#### Breach Configuration
- **Open breaches** — Spells can be prepped here immediately.
- **Closed breaches** — Must be focused (rotated) then opened before use; costs Aether.
- **Focus cost** — Each breach has a focus cost to rotate it one step.
- **Open cost** — Final focus step opens the breach permanently.

#### Starting Hand
- **Shuffle starting deck** — Shuffle your starting deck without looking.
- **Draw 5 cards** — Draw your opening hand of 5 cards.

--- Player Count Adjustments ---
- **1 Player** — Control 2 mages.
- **2 Players** — Standard; 1 mage each.
- **3-4 Players** — 1 mage each; nemesis HP may scale.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Nemesis setup effects** — Resolve any nemesis-specific setup instructions.
2. **Reveal turn order** — Draw the top card of the turn order deck to determine who goes first.
3. **Take turns** — Resolve turns as the turn order deck dictates.
4. **Begin play** — The first drawn player or nemesis takes their turn.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Order System
- **Variable turn order** — Shuffle a deck of turn order cards; draw one each turn to determine if a player or the nemesis acts.
- **Nemesis turn cards** — 2 nemesis turn cards in the deck.
- **Player turn cards** — 1 card per player.
- **Unpredictability** — You never know if the nemesis will act next or a player will.

#### Player Turn (3 phases)
1. **Casting phase** — Cast any prepped spells on open breaches (deal damage to nemesis or minions).
2. **Main phase** — Gain Aether from gems, play relics, prep new spells on breaches, focus/open breaches, buy new cards from supply.
3. **Draw phase** — Discard remaining hand cards to discard pile IN ANY ORDER YOU CHOOSE. Draw 5 new cards.

#### Nemesis Turn (2 phases)
1. **Main phase** — Draw and resolve the top nemesis deck card (attack, minion, or power).
2. **Strike phase** — Any unleashed powers and active minions resolve their persistent effects.

#### Key Rule: No Shuffling
- **Discard order matters** — Cards go to discard in the order you choose.
- **Deck cycles** — When your draw deck is empty, flip your discard pile to become your new draw deck WITHOUT SHUFFLING.
- **Strategic stacking** — Plan discard order to control future draws.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Main Phase Actions (any number, any order)
- **Play gems** — Play gem cards to gain their Aether value; discard after use.
- **Play relics** — Play relic cards for their effect; discard after use.
- **Prep a spell** — Place a spell card from hand onto an open breach (face up). It stays there until cast.
- **Focus a breach** — Pay the focus cost to rotate a closed breach one step closer to opening.
- **Open a breach** — Pay the open cost on a fully focused breach to open it permanently.
- **Buy a card** — Spend Aether to purchase a card from any supply pile; it goes on top of your discard pile.
- **Use charge ability** — Some mages accumulate charges and can spend them for a powerful unique ability.

#### Casting Spells
- **Cast timing** — Spells are cast during the casting phase at the start of your turn.
- **Damage dealing** — Most spells deal damage to the nemesis or a minion.
- **After casting** — Cast spells go to your discard pile (in the order you choose).
- **Breach bonus** — Some breaches grant bonus effects when spells are cast from them (e.g., +1 damage).

#### Buying Cards
- **Gem cards** — Improve your economy for future turns.
- **Spell cards** — Increase your damage output.
- **Relic cards** — Provide utility effects (healing, card draw, breach manipulation).
- **Placement** — Purchased cards go to the top of your discard pile.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### No-Shuffle Mechanic
- **Core innovation** — You never shuffle your discard pile; you control the order cards enter your discard.
- **Deck planning** — Discard cards strategically so your next hand cycle has the cards you need together.
- **Purchased card placement** — New cards go on top of discard, so you know when they will appear.

#### Breach System
- **4 breaches per mage** — Breaches are where spells are prepped.
- **Closed → focused → open** — Breaches must be rotated (focused) to eventually open permanently.
- **Investment** — Opening breaches costs Aether over multiple turns but enables more spells per casting phase.
- **Breach bonuses** — Later breaches often have innate bonuses (+1 damage, etc.).

#### Nemesis Mechanics
- **Minions** — Enter play and persist; deal damage each nemesis strike phase until destroyed.
- **Powers** — Countdown cards; if not discarded by dealing enough damage, they trigger devastating effects.
- **Attacks** — Immediate damage or harmful effects resolved when drawn.
- **Unique nemesis rules** — Each boss has a unique mechanic (e.g., spawning tokens, corrupting cards, shield phases).

#### Gravehold
- **Shared HP pool** — Gravehold starts at 30 HP; nemesis cards and effects can damage it.
- **Gravehold loss** — If Gravehold reaches 0 HP, all players lose.
- **Mage loss** — If any mage reaches 0 HP, that mage is exhausted (reduced actions). In some versions, the mage is eliminated.

#### Charge Abilities
- **Mage unique power** — Each mage has a charge ability requiring multiple charges to activate.
- **Gaining charges** — Specific cards and effects grant charges.
- **Powerful effects** — Charge abilities are often game-changing (mass damage, full heal, etc.).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
- **Defeat the nemesis** — Reduce the nemesis to 0 HP.

#### Loss Conditions
- **Gravehold destroyed** — Gravehold HP reaches 0.
- **All mages exhausted/eliminated** — All mages are reduced to 0 HP.
- **Nemesis-specific** — Some nemeses have unique loss conditions.

#### Scoring
- **No VP system** — Aeon's End is a binary win/loss cooperative game.
- **Difficulty scaling** — Harder nemeses, fewer supply options, and challenge modes increase difficulty.
- **Legacy/Campaign** — Some versions feature campaign progression with persistent upgrades.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Buy economy first** — Purchase a gem upgrade early to fund expensive spells later.
- **Open breaches strategically** — Focus on opening your third breach early; the fourth is expensive but powerful.
- **Plan discard order from turn 1** — Stack gems together and spells together for powerful future turns.
- **Read the nemesis** — Identify the nemesis's most dangerous cards and prepare counters.
- **Coordinate with teammates** — One player focuses economy while another prioritizes damage output.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Random discard order** — The no-shuffle mechanic rewards careful stacking; random discard wastes the advantage.
- **Over-investing in breaches** — Spending too much Aether focusing/opening breaches while the nemesis deals unchecked damage.
- **Ignoring minions** — Letting minions persist causes compounding damage each nemesis turn.
- **All damage, no economy** — Buying only spells without gem upgrades leads to slow, inefficient turns.
- **Neglecting Gravehold** — Gravehold damage sneaks up; healing Gravehold is sometimes more critical than dealing damage.
- **Forgetting power countdowns** — Unaddressed power cards trigger catastrophic effects.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Economy vs damage** — Whether to buy gems for long-term output or spells for immediate pressure.
- **Breach investment timing** — When to spend Aether opening breaches vs buying cards.
- **Target priority** — Focus nemesis HP directly or clear minions and prevent powers first.
- **Discard sequencing** — How to order your discard pile for optimal next-cycle hands.
- **Heal vs attack** — When to spend resources on healing mages or Gravehold vs pushing for the kill.
